ai_chat.query: What does this mean?  Below is a summary of selected scientific studies on the toxicity of Exafluoran®. Table 1 is a report from the Environmental Protection Agency on the safety of Exafluoran at varying concentrations. This is not a comprehensive list but includes all findings of published experimental studies with at least one replication. Note that this synopsis was based on a meta-data search in 2017. More studies meet those criteria every year and might change the risk assessment, in particular regarding the low concentration health risks.

response.content: The overview of the toxicity of Exafluoran® is derived from selected scientific studies summarized by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA).
The summary provides insights into the varying effects of Exafluoran based on its concentration in water, noting the possible health risks and properties at each level.

response.title: EPA Brochure on the Toxicity of Exafluoran
response.description: Detailed report on the concentration-based effects of Exafluoran, including properties and potential health risks at different levels.

content: Highlights include the potential for unexpected combustion at very high concentrations (10,000,000 ppb) and less severe effects such as dizziness and nausea at much lower concentrations (500 ppb).

response.content: The document emphasizes that the threshold levels and health impacts could change as new studies are conducted, reflecting ongoing research into the effects of low-level exposure.
